statutoryBodies.name: State Printing Corporation
statutoryBodies.statusNote: Established under Section 2 as a body corporate with perpetual succession. Serves as the chief printing and publication arm of the government, local bodies, and public corporations. Expanded in 1981 to include private commercial printing and in 1998 to cover import/export of books and stationery.
powers: Carry out printing and publishing for government, local bodies, and public corporations (Section 4)
Control the printing of school textbooks and educational aids (Section 4)
Engage in commercial printing for private institutions and individuals (1981 Amendment)
Sell, distribute, and export books and educational aids (1998 Amendment)
Import books, newspapers, magazines, and educational aids (1998 Amendment)
Co-publish materials with other entities (1998 Amendment)
Manufacture, import, export, purchase, and sell stationery and ancillary products (1998 Amendment)
Acquire, hold, lease, pawn, or sell property (Section 5)
dataGaps: Current annual revenue and production volume
Percentage of work dedicated to government vs. commercial printing
Current textbook printing contracts and volumes

statutoryBodies.name: Board of Directors (State Printing Corporation)
statutoryBodies.statusNote: The members of the Board of Directors function as the members of the Corporation itself (Section 3). The Board administers all affairs of the Corporation (Section 14) and may delegate duties (Section 15). Chairman appointed under Section 7/16.

exOfficio.note: Appointed under Sections 7/16; has casting vote (Section 11)

nominated.note: Appointed to the Board (Section 7); must disclose personal interest in contracts (Section 9)

meetings.reporting: Annual report, auditor's statement, and statistics to the Minister, laid before Parliament (Section 25)

powers: Administer all affairs of the Corporation (Section 14)
Appoint staff, officers, servants, and agents (Section 26)
Custody of the Corporation's official seal (Section 17)
dataGaps: Current Board composition — names and backgrounds of Directors
Number of Directors on the Board
Meeting frequency and recent meeting dates

amendments.summary: Early structural adjustments to the Corporation's mandate, adjusting its interaction with growing state education requirements.
amendments.details: Made early structural adjustments to the Corporation's mandate, adjusting its interaction with the rapidly growing state education requirements. This preceded the more significant 1981 expansion of the Corporation's commercial powers.

amendments.summary: Expanded Section 4 to allow the Corporation to engage in commercial printing for private institutions and individuals.
amendments.details: Expanded the 'General Objects' (Section 4) to officially allow the Corporation to engage in commercial printing and publishing for private institutions and individuals. Added a stipulation that private commercial requests must never disrupt or delay the priority printing of school textbooks and educational aids.

amendments.summary: Further amended Section 4 to authorize import/export of books, co-publication, and manufacture/sale of stationery.
amendments.details: Modernized the Corporation's commercial capabilities by authorizing: sale, distribution, and export of books and educational aids; import of books, newspapers, magazines, and educational aids; co-publication with other entities; and manufacture, import, export, purchase, and sale of stationery and ancillary products. Transformed the Corporation from a government printer into a broader commercial supplier.

timeline.details: Act No. 24 of 1968 established the State Printing Corporation as a body corporate to serve as the chief printing and publication arm of the government. 32 sections across 4 parts covering constitution, finance, employees, and general provisions.
Early structural adjustments to the Corporation's mandate, adjusting its interaction with growing state education requirements.
Expanded Section 4 to allow commercial printing for private institutions and individuals, with a stipulation that government and educational printing takes priority.
Major commercial modernisation: authorized import/export of books, co-publication, and manufacture/sale of stationery. Transformed the Corporation into a broader commercial supplier.

tiers.name: Minister (Mass Communication/Education)
tiers.relationship: Overriding written directives to the Board (Section 6); makes regulations (Section 29); receives annual report (Section 25)

tiers.name: Board of Directors
tiers.relationship: Administers all affairs of the Corporation (Section 14); Chairman appointed under Section 7/16; quorum of 3

tiers.name: State Printing Corporation (Operations)
tiers.relationship: Body corporate executing printing, publishing, and commercial operations

currentReplacement.national: State Printing Corporation (body corporate under Board of Directors)
